title: Project XC Quantum Chemistry Academy
description: A source-grounded, interactive path from first quantum ideas to professor-level electronic-structure theory and research practice.

tracks.title: Foundations
tracks.description: Build the mathematical and physical language required by every later electronic-structure method.

chapters.title: Quantum Foundations
chapters.summary: Learn states, probability, phase, operators, stationary states, uncertainty, spin, and the bridge to many-electron quantum chemistry.
outcomes: Interpret a wavefunction without treating it as a classical trajectory
Connect operators and eigenvalues to measurable quantities
Use simple solvable models while stating their limitations

chapters.title: Mathematical Language
chapters.summary: Master complex vectors, inner products, matrices, basis changes, tensor products, Fourier pairs, and variational calculus through visual problems.
outcomes: Translate between vector, function, and matrix representations
Recognize Hermitian and unitary transformations
Follow the linear algebra used by electronic-structure algorithms

chapters.title: Approximation and Variational Thinking
chapters.summary: Learn how variational bounds, perturbative corrections, finite representations, dimensional analysis, and explicit error budgets make difficult quantum problems solvable.
outcomes: Choose an approximation based on its assumptions
Distinguish truncation error from model error
Use variational and perturbative reasoning responsibly

tracks.title: Atoms, Orbitals, and Representations
tracks.description: Move from one-electron atoms to finite basis representations, symmetry, and molecular-orbital construction.

chapters.title: Atomic Structure
chapters.summary: Explore hydrogenic orbitals, quantum numbers, radial and angular nodes, degeneracy, many-electron shells, and the periodic structure of atoms.
outcomes: Relate quantum numbers to orbital structure
Count and interpret radial and angular nodes
Separate exact hydrogenic results from many-electron models

chapters.title: Basis Sets and Integral Engines
chapters.summary: Use the existing Basis Set Quest to learn Gaussian functions, contractions, conditioning, basis strategy, analytic integrals, screening, recurrences, and derivatives.
outcomes: Connect basis flexibility to chemical accuracy and cost
Diagnose diffuse-function and linear-dependence risks
Explain how Gaussian integral engines build Hamiltonian matrices

chapters.title: Molecular Orbitals and Symmetry
chapters.summary: Use the existing MO theory guide and builder to study LCAO mixing, symmetry, phase, degeneracy, active spaces, and chemically meaningful orbital interpretation.
outcomes: Construct qualitative MO diagrams from symmetry and energy
Treat degenerate orbitals as rotatable subspaces
Choose active orbitals using character rather than picture alone

tracks.title: Many-Electron and Mean-Field Theory
tracks.description: Build antisymmetric many-electron states and derive the Hartree–Fock and self-consistent-field machinery used throughout quantum chemistry.

chapters.title: Many-Electron Wavefunctions
chapters.summary: Learn indistinguishability, antisymmetry, spin orbitals, Slater determinants, selection rules, and reduced density matrices before using mean-field approximations.
outcomes: Apply antisymmetry under electron exchange
Build and compare Slater determinants
Connect determinant matrix elements to selection rules

chapters.title: Hartree–Fock and SCF
chapters.summary: Derive Coulomb and exchange, the Fock operator, Roothaan–Hall equations, density matrices, SCF convergence strategies, and restricted or open-shell reference choices.
outcomes: Derive the self-consistent Hartree–Fock equations
Diagnose common SCF convergence failures
Distinguish orbital representations from observables

chapters.title: Geometry, Gradients, and Frequencies
chapters.summary: Connect electronic energies to analytic gradients, Pulay terms, optimization algorithms, Hessians, normal modes, transition states, and reaction paths.
outcomes: Interpret gradients and Hessian eigenvectors
Choose an optimization method for the surface topology
Distinguish minima, transition states, and numerical artifacts

tracks.title: Density-Functional Theory
tracks.description: Understand why density methods work, how Kohn–Sham equations are constructed, and where approximate functionals succeed or fail.

chapters.title: Density and Kohn–Sham DFT
chapters.summary: Follow the density-based logic from Hohenberg–Kohn ideas through the Kohn–Sham construction, exchange-correlation energy, potentials, and density self-consistency.
outcomes: Distinguish interacting density theory from its Kohn–Sham representation
Explain the role of the exchange-correlation functional
Recognize density-driven and functional-driven errors

chapters.title: Functional Families and Failures
chapters.summary: Connect Project XC’s live catalog to Jacob’s ladder, exact exchange, range separation, dispersion, self-interaction, delocalization error, and method-selection diagnostics.
outcomes: Compare functional ingredients rather than names alone
Identify major qualitative DFT failure modes
Trace a functional claim to its source and implementation

tracks.title: Electron Correlation
tracks.description: Progress from correlation holes and determinant spaces through perturbation, coupled cluster, and multireference electronic structure.

chapters.title: Correlation Foundations
chapters.summary: Learn correlation holes, dynamic versus static correlation, excitation spaces, size consistency, size extensivity, and basis convergence before choosing a correlated method.
outcomes: Distinguish dynamic and static correlation regimes
Explain size consistency and size extensivity
Diagnose when a single determinant is inadequate

chapters.title: Perturbation Theory and Configuration Interaction
chapters.summary: Build MP and CI approximations from excitation ranks, amplitudes, denominators, determinant dimensions, intruder-state risks, and size-consistency behavior.
outcomes: Construct excitation spaces by rank
Recognize dangerous perturbative denominators
Explain why truncated CI is not size consistent

chapters.title: Coupled-Cluster Theory
chapters.summary: Understand the exponential ansatz, connected terms, CCSD, perturbative triples, equation-of-motion extensions, diagnostics, and computational scaling.
outcomes: Relate exponential amplitudes to disconnected products
Explain size-extensive coupled-cluster behavior
Use diagnostics without treating them as universal verdicts

chapters.title: Multireference Methods
chapters.summary: Learn active spaces, CASSCF orbital and configuration optimization, state averaging, CASPT2, NEVPT2, MRCI, intruder states, and active-space diagnostics.
outcomes: Choose an active space from orbital and state character
Distinguish state-specific and state-averaged optimization
Recognize intruder and root-tracking problems

tracks.title: Excited States and Dynamics
tracks.description: Treat electronic excitation, state character, conical intersections, nonadiabatic coupling, and time-dependent molecular behavior.

chapters.title: Excited-State Methods
chapters.summary: Compare CIS, TDDFT, TDA, EOM methods, spin-flip ideas, transition properties, state character, and known limitations for double or charge-transfer excitations.
outcomes: Match an excited-state method to state character
Interpret transition moments and oscillator strengths
Track states by character rather than energy order alone

chapters.title: Conical Intersections and Nonadiabatic Coupling
chapters.summary: Explore adiabatic and diabatic representations, avoided crossings, branching planes, derivative couplings, Berry phase, and nonadiabatic transition models.
outcomes: Distinguish avoided crossings from conical intersections
Interpret the branching plane and derivative couplings
Recognize representation and phase-convention issues

chapters.title: Quantum and Nonadiabatic Dynamics
chapters.summary: Connect time propagation, wavepackets, autocorrelation spectra, Ehrenfest dynamics, surface hopping, decoherence, and higher-dimensional quantum dynamics.
outcomes: Relate time correlation functions to spectra
Compare mean-field and trajectory hopping approximations
Identify when decoherence and nuclear quantum effects matter

tracks.title: Environment and Molecular Properties
tracks.description: Connect electronic structure to solvent response, spectroscopy, magnetic properties, and analytic response observables.

chapters.title: Solvation and Embedding
chapters.summary: Learn continuum electrostatics, cavity construction, PCM and COSMO ideas, explicit solvent, QM/MM, polarizable embedding, and equilibrium or nonequilibrium response.
outcomes: Separate electrostatic, cavitation, and specific-solvent effects
Choose an environment model for the target property
Recognize cavity and response-model sensitivity

chapters.title: Spectroscopy
chapters.summary: Connect calculated states and response properties to IR, Raman, UV–visible, fluorescence, phosphorescence, vibronic structure, EPR, and nonlinear spectroscopy.
outcomes: Apply electric-dipole and vibrational selection rules
Relate potential surfaces to vibronic structure
Distinguish vertical, adiabatic, and emission energies

chapters.title: Magnetic and Response Properties
chapters.summary: Develop perturbation response, polarizabilities, hyperpolarizabilities, NMR shielding, gauge-origin handling, EPR tensors, and analytic response equations.
outcomes: Connect property derivatives to response equations
Explain gauge-origin problems and GIAO strategies
Distinguish relaxed and unrelaxed electronic response

tracks.title: Professor and Research Frontier
tracks.description: Study relativistic Hamiltonians, electronic-structure algorithms, Green’s functions, advanced correlation, quantum computing, and rigorous research practice.

chapters.title: Relativistic Quantum Chemistry
chapters.summary: Understand scalar relativistic effects, spin–orbit coupling, effective core potentials, DKH and X2C transformations, four-component theory, and picture-change effects.
outcomes: Identify chemical signatures of scalar and spin–orbit effects
Choose a relativistic Hamiltonian at an appropriate cost
Recognize picture-change and basis compatibility issues

chapters.title: Electronic-Structure Algorithms
chapters.summary: Learn integral-direct methods, DIIS and Davidson solvers, sparse and local approximations, density fitting, tensor contractions, parallelism, and memory-versus-flop trade-offs.
outcomes: Estimate memory and operation scaling
Choose iterative eigensolver and SCF acceleration strategies
Connect mathematical sparsity to software implementation

chapters.title: Green’s Functions and Quasiparticles
chapters.summary: Explore propagators, poles, Dyson equations and orbitals, self-energies, spectral functions, GW, ADC, and electron addition or removal pictures.
outcomes: Interpret spectral poles and quasiparticle weights
Connect self-energy structure to electron correlation
Distinguish orbital coefficients from Dyson amplitudes

chapters.title: Local Correlation, F12, and Embedding
chapters.summary: Study orbital domains, pair natural orbitals, explicit-correlation cusp repair, fragment embedding, and the thresholds controlling modern reduced-scaling methods.
outcomes: Explain how locality reduces correlated-method cost
Connect F12 terms to electron-electron cusp behavior
Audit threshold and fragment errors separately

chapters.title: Quantum Computing for Chemistry
chapters.summary: Connect second quantization to qubit mappings, ansätze, VQE, phase estimation, Pauli measurement cost, error mitigation, and realistic hardware resource limits.
outcomes: Map simple fermionic operators to qubit operators
Separate algorithmic scaling from hardware overhead
Evaluate quantum advantage claims using explicit resources

chapters.title: Research Practice and Benchmark Design
chapters.summary: Design reproducible benchmarks, match states and roots, separate error sources, preserve provenance, report uncertainty, diagnose failed calculations, and publish negative results honestly.
outcomes: Design a benchmark with defensible references
Separate numerical, model, and assignment errors
Report calculations with reproducible provenance
